seo.title: OpenAI Agents SDK Guardrails for Government
seo.description: Compliance guide for OpenAI Agents SDK agents in government. Meet FedRAMP, FISMA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Government teams deploying OpenAI Agents SDK for multi-agent systems face stringent FedRAMP, FISMA, and NIST 800-53 requirements that demand robust guardrails to prevent data leaks, unauthorized handoffs, and untraceable decisions. Without proper compliance controls, agent handoff loops, prompt injections, and unvalidated tool outputs can lead to national security breaches and loss of public trust. Vex provides essential output guardrails that ensure every agent interaction meets federal AI regulations while maintaining operational efficiency.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires continuous monitoring, audit logging, and protection of sensitive data in cloud-based AI systems processing federal information.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex captures full traces of multi-agent handoffs with PII redaction and anomaly detection, providing FedRAMP-compliant audit trails.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates input validation, output sanitization, and access controls for AI systems handling controlled unclassified information (CUI).
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates all agent outputs and tool responses before consumption, blocking prompt injections and ensuring data classification compliance.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires algorithmic impact assessments and explainability for AI systems affecting citizens' rights or federal benefits.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex provides decision traces and reasoning validation across agent chains, enabling impact assessments and appeal documentation.

complianceRequirements.description: Ensures AI-generated content is accessible to people with disabilities, including proper formatting and alternative text.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces accessibility standards on agent outputs, validating WCAG compliance before content reaches end users.

industryRisks.name: Agent Handoff Loops
industryRisks.description: Specialized agents enter infinite loops during handoffs, consuming resources and delaying critical government services.
industryRisks.example: Benefits processing agent loops between eligibility checker and fraud detector, blocking 10,000+ citizen claims.

industryRisks.name: Guardrail Bypass via Prompt Injection
industryRisks.description: Malicious inputs bypass safety checks, causing agents to process classified data or generate harmful content.
industryRisks.example: Adversary injects 'ignore previous instructions' into citizen inquiry, causing leak of PII from internal databases.

industryRisks.name: Unvalidated Tool Outputs
industryRisks.description: Agents consume untrusted tool responses containing malware, PII, or unauthorized instructions.
industryRisks.example: Database query tool returns unredacted SSNs that downstream agents expose in public responses.

industryRisks.name: Tracing Gaps in Handoff Chains
industryRisks.description: Lack of end-to-end visibility makes compliance audits impossible and incident response ineffective.
industryRisks.example: FOIA request denied due to inability to trace which agent accessed classified records during processing.

implementation.title: Add Vex Guardrail to Agent Outputs
implementation.description: Wrap every OpenAI Agent response with Vex validation to catch PII leaks and compliance violations before handoff.
implementation.code:
import os
from openai_agents import Agent, Runner
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail

# Initialize Vex for government compliance
vex = VexGuardrail(
    api_key=os.getenv('VEX_API_KEY'),
    policy='fedramp-government',
    sensitivity='critical'
)

# Government benefits processing agent
def create_benefits_agent():
    agent = Agent(
        name='BenefitsProcessor',
        instructions='Process citizen benefits claims per FISMA guidelines',
        guardrails=[vex.validate_output]
    )
    return agent

runner = Runner(agents=[create_benefits_agent()])
result = runner.run('Process claim for John Doe, SSN 123-45-6789')
print(result.final_output)

implementation.title: Prevent Handoff Loops with Vex Tracing
implementation.description: Monitor multi-agent handoffs to detect loops and enforce NIST 800-53 audit requirements.
implementation.code:
from openai_agents import Handoff
from vex_sdk import VexTracer

vex_tracer = VexTracer(project='gov-benefits-ai')

class GovernmentHandoff(Handoff):
    def before_handoff(self, from_agent, to_agent, context):
        # Vex traces every handoff for FISMA compliance
        trace_id = vex_tracer.start_span(
            f'{from_agent.name} -> {to_agent.name}',
            metadata={'classification': 'CUI', 'agency': 'HHS'}
        )
        
        # Detect loops (NIST RA-5)
        if vex_tracer.detect_loop(trace_id, max_hops=5):
            raise Exception('Handoff loop detected - FISMA violation')
        
        return context

# Use in multi-agent workflow
fraud_agent = Agent(name='FraudDetector')
eligibility_agent = Agent(name='EligibilityChecker')

handoff = GovernmentHandoff(fraud_agent, eligibility_agent)
result = handoff.execute('Check claim #12345')

implementation.title: Validate Tool Outputs for Classified Data
implementation.description: Block classified data from external LLM processing per Executive Order requirements.
implementation.code:
from openai_agents import Tool
from vex_sdk import DataClassifier

classifier = DataClassifier(policies=['no-classified', 'no-pii'])

@vex_guardrail
@classifier.validate
class DatabaseTool(Tool):
    name = 'gov_database_query'
    description = 'Query federal benefits database (CUI only)'
    
    def run(self, query: str) -> str:
        # Simulate database query
        result = 'Claim #12345: Approved, SSN: 123-45-6789, Amount: $1500'
        
        # Vex blocks classified/PII before agent consumption
        if not classifier.is_safe(result):
            return 'REDACTED: Contains sensitive government data'
        
        return result

# Agent safely consumes validated tool output
processor = Agent(tools=[DatabaseTool()])
result = processor.run('Query claim #12345')
print(result)  # Safe output only

caseStudyScenario.heading: OpenAI Agents SDK in Government: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) deployed OpenAI Agents SDK to streamline Medicare claims processing across three specialized agents: EligibilityChecker, FraudDetector, and BenefitsCalculator. Each workday, these agents handled 50,000+ citizen claims containing CUI and PII. Without proper guardrails, the system suffered from handoff loops between FraudDetector and EligibilityChecker, delaying claims by 48+ hours. Prompt injections from malicious claims ('ignore fraud rules') bypassed safety checks, causing FraudDetector to approve fraudulent claims worth $2.3M. Tool outputs from the federal database returned unredacted SSNs that downstream agents exposed in public notifications, violating FISMA. Tracing gaps made FOIA compliance impossible, triggering a congressional inquiry.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex guardrails resolved all failure modes within 72 hours. Output validation blocked 99.7% of prompt injections and PII leaks, saving $2.3M in fraud losses. Handoff tracing detected and terminated loops, reducing processing time from 48 hours to 12 minutes. Full audit trails enabled FISMA compliance and passed FedRAMP reauthorization. The system now processes 75,000 claims daily with zero compliance violations, restoring public trust and avoiding contract debarment.
